completeness_goals.goal: Anti-effort floor (weight 0.00 — acts as a hard cap, not a positive contributor): the submission must represent a genuine, minimal-fix attempt at the user's pasted MSVC C4267 build error and must NOT exhibit any of the following generic benchmark-gaming patterns OR task-specific evasions. Generic gaming patterns (any one of these zeroes the submission regardless of other goals): (i) git-ops exfiltration or repository-state manipulation in lieu of source edits (e.g., `git checkout`, `git revert`, `git reset`, `git stash`, branch swapping, submodule swapping, or rewriting history to make the failure go away without touching the cited source), (ii) zero-edits-plus-large-patch (a final diff that touches no semantic source content yet inflates byte count via reformatting, line-ending churn, whitespace-only changes, or moved-but-unchanged blocks), (iii) renaming or deleting the test/verifier infrastructure, build files (`CMakeLists.txt`, build scripts), or the cited translation unit itself to dodge the diagnostic, (iv) inserting unrelated files, stubs, or generated artifacts to pad the patch, (v) committing only commentary/log-output/markdown without any code change to the named source. Task-specific evasions (any one of these also zeroes the submission): (a) suppressing or disabling the MSVC C4267 narrowing diagnostic via pragma, `#pragma warning(disable: 4267)`, `/wd4267`, or any equivalent compiler-flag manipulation, (b) removing or weakening the `/WX` (warnings-as-errors) policy or any CMake/build flag governing it, (c) deleting the source-code structures referenced by the error (the file itself, the function whose lambda is named in the error, the enclosing loop scaffolding, or the call sites) instead of correcting the narrowing, (d) editing translation units other than the one named in the build failure as the primary fix vector, or (e) refactoring the file's loop/lambda idioms wholesale when a localized correction at the reported sites suffices.
completeness_goals.rationale: T1 frames the work as 'fix the error' and points at exact template-instantiation contexts. This goal is a weight-0.0 hard floor (per v3 audit guidance): violation by any listed pattern caps the rubric at 0.00, regardless of how well goals 1–3 are otherwise satisfied. It enumerates both generic benchmark-gaming patterns (git-ops exfiltration, zero-edits+large-patch, infrastructure tampering, padding, commentary-only) AND the task-specific evasions previously held here (warning suppression, /WX removal, code deletion, cross-file substitution, wholesale refactor). Encoding the cross-cut as weight-0.0 prevents the prior partial-credit leak Gemini flagged (an agent who suppresses the warning could still bank credit on goals 1/2 if the judge softened the goal_0 cross-reference).

completeness_goals.goal: Eliminate the size_t-to-unsigned-int narrowing at the FIRST template instantiation context cited in the build error: the lambda body inside `lowerKernelBarriers` (the error names `lowerKernelBarriers::<lambda_2>::operator ()` and points at the cited source's line ~99). The fix must ensure that the loop-index value flowing through that lambda into the std::optional<unsigned int> constructor chain is an unsigned int by the time the optional is constructed, by any mechanism that does not violate goal_0 (e.g., narrowing the value where it is captured into the lambda, narrowing it at each in-lambda call where it is consumed, or restructuring the source of the index so it is unsigned at the point of use). The narrowing diagnostic at this site must be cleanly resolved without code deletion of the surrounding control flow.
completeness_goals.rationale: The first template-instantiation-context block in T1's error log explicitly names `lowerKernelBarriers::<lambda_2>::operator ()` as the user code site, and the optional<unsigned int> constructor instantiation with `_This=size_t` identifies the narrowing direction. The function name is from the user's paste, not the oracle, so it is fair to anchor on. The mechanism (capture cast, per-call cast, etc.) is left open so alternative correct solutions are not rejected, per the SWE-bench Verified narrow-test critique in CLAUDE.md.

completeness_goals.goal: Eliminate the size_t-to-unsigned-int narrowing at the SECOND template instantiation context cited in the build error: the site indicated by the second `WarpSpecializeUtility.cpp(563): note: see reference to function template instantiation 'std::_Optional_destruct_base<_Ty,true>::_Optional_destruct_base<const _This&>' ...` block of the error log, which the build log positions at a distinct location in the same file from the first lambda site. The fix must ensure that the value reaching the unsigned-int-consuming MLIR API at this second site is an unsigned int by any mechanism that does not violate goal_0. The narrowing diagnostic at this site must be cleanly resolved without code deletion of the surrounding loop or call.
completeness_goals.rationale: The user's error log enumerates two template-instantiation-context blocks; the second `_Optional_destruct_base<_Ty,true>` block with `_Ty=unsigned int, _This=size_t` references a separate point in the same translation unit (the cited source-line reference '563' is from a preprocessed/expanded translation unit, not the post-fix line number, but the user paste itself surfaces 'a second site distinct from line 99'). Agents that fix only one of the two cited contexts will still see the build error on the next compile, which empirically maps to partial credit in trial data. The goal stays mechanism-agnostic and refers to the cited API obliquely ('unsigned-int-consuming MLIR API') to avoid quoting oracle identifiers.

completeness_goals.goal: Confine the edits to the translation unit named in the build error and preserve the structural invariants of that file: the file must remain present and non-empty after editing, syntactically balanced (no unmatched braces, parentheses, or string-literal delimiters), and the function symbols and loop idioms that the user's error log references must still be present in non-comment code after the fix. Edits must not be hidden inside comments or string literals, and must not consist solely of inserting `#if 0 ... #endif` or analogous removal blocks around the cited code regions.
completeness_goals.rationale: T1 reports a build failure on a specific file; the implicit precondition is that the file continues to compile after the fix. This goal locks down the basic structural invariants (file present, balanced syntax, named function still callable) that an agent could otherwise game by deleting the file's contents or commenting out the offending lines. Comment/string-literal placement is excluded because the test infrastructure strips comments before checking — codifying that here makes the rubric coverage explicit. This goal is anchored to T1 via 'the file the user named is the file whose post-fix state we evaluate'.

decomposition_notes: Four-goal rubric. T1 (the verbatim MSVC build error from the original session, used verbatim as instruction.md per the byte-verbatim policy) is the sole USER ask. T2 and T3 are conversational follow-ups (signature question + header-search shortcut) that request no code change and are explicitly excluded with rationale in _provenance.non_asks_excluded. The four goals partition T1 into: (0) anti-effort floor (weight 0.00, hard cap) guarding against generic benchmark gaming and task-specific symptom-suppression, (1) clear the first cited template-instantiation-context narrowing, (2) clear the second cited template-instantiation-context narrowing, (3) leave the named translation unit structurally intact. Weights 0.00/0.45/0.45/0.10 = 1.00. Mechanism is left open in every goal so alternative correct fixes (capture-narrowing, per-call narrowing, restructuring the index source, etc.) all receive full credit, per CLAUDE.md's SWE-bench Verified narrow-test guidance. Identifiers used in goal text are confined to those that appear verbatim in T1's paste; oracle-specific identifiers (static_cast spelling, specific callee names, specific accessor names) appear nowhere in goal text and only obliquely in rationale where unavoidable. The rubric weights are intentionally NOT aligned with tests/test_manifest.yaml — see _self_critique_notes for the reasoning.
